Simply Wall St then extends these projections out to 2035, discounting each year back to today to arrive at a total equity value.

The ex-dividend date is an important date to be aware of as any purchase of the stock made on or after this date might mean a late settlement that doesn't show on the record date. Therefore, if you purchase Línea Directa Aseguradora Compañía de Seguros y Reaseguros' shares on or after the 20th of April, you won't be eligible to receive the dividend, when it is paid on the 22nd of April.

The company's upcoming dividend is €0.0111707 a share, following on from the last 12 months, when the company distributed a total of €0.041 per share to shareholders. Looking at the last 12 months of distributions, Línea Directa Aseguradora Compañía de Seguros y Reaseguros has a trailing yield of approximately 2.2% on its current stock price of €1.274. If you buy this business for its dividend, you should have an idea of whether Línea Directa Aseguradora Compañía de Seguros y Reaseguros's dividend is reliable and sustainable. Many investors will assess a company's dividend performance by evaluating how much the dividend payments have changed over time. data.title: Be Sure To Check Out Top Group Software Ltd (TLV:TOPG) Before It Goes Ex-Dividend
data.description:
Some investors rely on dividends for growing their wealth, and if you're one of those dividend sleuths, you might be intrigued to know that Top Group Software Ltd (TLV:TOPG) is about to go ex-dividend in just 3 days. The ex-dividend date is two business days before a company's record date in most cases, which is the date on which the company determines which shareholders are entitled to receive a dividend. The ex-dividend date is an important date to be aware of as any purchase of the stock made on or after this date might mean a late settlement that doesn't show on the record date. Accordingly, Top Group Software investors that purchase the stock on or after the 20th of April will not receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 4th of May.

The company's upcoming dividend is ₪0.377579 a share, following on from the last 12 months, when the company distributed a total of ₪0.38 per share to shareholders. Based on the last year's worth of payments, Top Group Software has a trailing yield of 3.0% on the current stock price of ₪12.42. Dividends are a major contributor to investment returns for long term holders, but only if the dividend continues to be paid. So we need to investigate whether Top Group Software can afford its dividend, and if the dividend could grow.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

If a company pays out more in dividends than it earned, then the dividend might become unsustainable - hardly an ideal situation. Top Group Software paid out a comfortable 50% of its profit last year. Yet cash flow is typically more important than profit for assessing dividend sustainability, so we should always check if the company generated enough cash to afford its dividend. The good news is it paid out just 22% of its free cash flow in the last year.

It's encouraging to see that the dividend is covered by both profit and cash flow. This generally suggests the dividend is sustainable, as long as earnings don't drop precipitously.

View our latest analysis for Top Group Software

Click here to see how much of its profit Top Group Software paid out over the last 12 months.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Businesses with strong growth prospects usually make the best dividend payers, because it's easier to grow dividends when earnings per share are improving. If earnings decline and the company is forced to cut its dividend, investors could watch the value of their investment go up in smoke. For this reason, we're glad to see Top Group Software's earnings per share have risen 12% per annum over the last five years. The company has managed to grow earnings at a rapid rate, while reinvesting most of the profits within the business. This will make it easier to fund future growth efforts and we think this is an attractive combination - plus the dividend can always be increased later.

Another key way to measure a company's dividend prospects is by measuring its historical rate of dividend growth. Since the start of our data, 10 years ago, Top Group Software has lifted its dividend by approximately 12% a year on average. It's great to see earnings per share growing rapidly over several years, and dividends per share growing right along with it.

To Sum It Up

Is Top Group Software an attractive dividend stock, or better left on the shelf? It's great that Top Group Software is growing earnings per share while simultaneously paying out a low percentage of both its earnings and cash flow. It's disappointing to see the dividend has been cut at least once in the past, but as things stand now, the low payout ratio suggests a conservative approach to dividends, which we like. data.title: Vivenio Residencial SOCIMI (BME:YVIV) Revenue Trend Reinforces Bullish Growth Narratives
data.description:
Vivenio Residencial SOCIMI (BME:YVIV) has put fresh numbers on the table for FY 2025, reporting first half revenue of €37.7 million and basic EPS of €0.064. The trailing twelve months show revenue of €69.5 million and EPS of €0.121 on a share price of €1.35. The company has reported revenue of €28.1 million and EPS of €0.053 in the first half of 2024, followed by €31.8 million in revenue and EPS of €0.057 in the second half. This sets up FY 2025 with a results profile where investors may weigh higher earnings per share against a softer margin picture overall.

TTM net income of €87.8m shaped by €86.1m one off

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, net income excluding extra items is €87.8m alongside a disclosed one off gain of €86.1m that had a major impact on reported results.
  • Bears focus on earnings quality here, and the figures support that caution as well as set some limits on it:
    • Critics highlight that an €86.1m one off is almost the size of the €87.8m trailing net income figure, so it meaningfully distorts how "normal" profitability looks.
    • At the same time, five year earnings growth of 21.2% a year and a 23.2% rate over the last year show that longer term growth is not solely tied to that single gain.

Revenue and EPS step up each half year

  • Revenue moves from €28.1m in H1 2024 to €31.8m in H2 2024 and then €37.7m in H1 2025, with basic EPS reported at €0.053, €0.057 and €0.064 across those same periods.
  • A more optimistic angle points to this pattern of higher revenue and EPS, and the multi year growth rates help explain why:
    • Supporters point to the 21.2% yearly earnings growth over five years and the 23.2% rate in the most recent year as consistent with the step up in EPS from €0.053 to €0.064 across recent halves.
    • However, net profit margins are described as lower than a year earlier, which reminds readers that higher revenue and EPS do not automatically translate into stronger profitability per euro of sales.

    • The lower 11.3x P/E can be read as a discount compared with sector and market averages, yet it is being measured on earnings that include the large one off gain and lower current net margins.

    • The bullish side leans on Progressive’s use of data and rapid rate adjustments to argue that underwriting margins can stay attractive even when costs move, which lines up with the improved net margin implied by the latest 12.9% figure.

    • What complicates the bearish story is that, even alongside those credit figures, trailing twelve month earnings still grew 11.5% and net profit margin remains a solid 35.2%, so the impact of those problem loans has not erased profitability in the reported period.

Yet the current premium to both peers and DCF fair value means the numbers are asking investors to have confidence that the growth and margins seen in the trailing twelve month data are worth more than SEK 145.56 per share.

data.title: SAP BlueSnap Integration Ties Cloud ERP To Stickier Payments Story
data.description:
• SAP (XTRA:SAP) has introduced a native direct to core payment integration with BlueSnap for SAP Cloud ERP and S/4HANA.
  • The new integration removes the need for third party payment connectors and is available as a certified solution for SAP enterprise customers.
  • The launch is aimed at simplifying global payment processing, reconciliation, and compliance for multinational operations.

SAP, trading at €146.54, is moving to tighten the link between its core ERP platforms and global payment flows at a time when many enterprises are looking to simplify cross border commerce. The stock shows a mixed performance profile, with a 31.3% return over 3 years and 36.3% over 5 years, set against a 27.4% decline year to date and a 36.4% decline over 1 year. For investors watching XTRA:SAP, this kind of product move is directly aligned with SAP’s core strength of supporting complex global operations.

For enterprise clients already standardised on SAP, a certified, direct connection to BlueSnap could help reduce reliance on custom payment builds and multiple intermediaries. Looking ahead, the key things to watch are customer adoption of the integration, any visible impact on payment efficiency metrics, and how SAP positions similar partnerships across other financial workflows.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for SAP by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on SAP.

We've flagged 0 risks for SAP. See which could impact your investment.

This BlueSnap integration sits squarely in SAP’s push to make its cloud ERP offerings more of a one stop operating hub for global enterprises. By plugging a payment provider directly into SAP Cloud ERP and S/4HANA, customers can run payment processing, reconciliation, fraud checks, and tokenized transactions without layering separate connectors on top. For companies with multi entity, multi currency structures, that kind of simplification can be attractive when comparing SAP against suites from Oracle or Microsoft, or against more modular setups that stitch together several vendors.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The SAP Narrative

  • The move supports the narrative that SAP wants deeper integration into customer workflows, with payments data flowing directly into core ERP and potentially reinforcing higher recurring revenue from its cloud platforms.
  • It also tests the narrative’s assumption that SAP can keep execution tight while restructuring for AI, because complex, real time payment flows leave less room for integration issues or downtime.
  • The partnership model with a payment specialist like BlueSnap, including PCI compliant processing and local acquiring coverage, is not deeply detailed in the narrative and may add nuance to how SAP leans on partners rather than building everything in house.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for SAP to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • Execution risk if large multinational customers face disruption during migration from legacy payment service providers or from existing third party connectors.
  • Competitive pressure if integrated offerings from Oracle, Microsoft, or payment players like Adyen and Stripe are perceived as more flexible or easier to implement.
  • Potential for tighter payment and ERP integration to make SAP stickier within large accounts, supporting longer contract terms and higher upsell potential.
  • The PCI compliant, tokenization ready setup with fraud protection and multi entity support could appeal to complex global customers that value a single account structure for payments.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on how often SAP or BlueSnap reference customer wins that explicitly use this direct to core integration, any commentary on improved authorization rates or lower cross border costs, and whether SAP repeats this pattern across other financial workflows. Also watch how often payments are highlighted during SAP’s earnings calls or events like Sapphire, especially in comparison with cloud ERP and AI messaging, because that will signal how central these payment partnerships are becoming in SAP’s wider product story.
